requires certification that the PA is promoting peace with Israel
Document 2: U.S. policy that no funds may be expended for projects prohibiting Israeli participation
prohibits discrimination against Israeli organizations
Document 3: includes Israel in the visa waiver program
determination that Israel has complied with visa waiver requirements

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Public Welfare
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing public welfare through environmental protection, education enrichment, and healthcare services.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to require...an oil spill containment and cleanup plan
Allows approval by the Administrator only if the applicant has demonstrated adequate technology, organization, resources, and capacity...
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to local educational agencies...
Gives priority to LEAs that have the greatest numbers or percentages of children from families below the poverty line.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to include recreational therapy services...
Determining whether inpatient services in an inpatient rehabilitation facility are reasonable and necessary.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting tax credits, healthcare mandates, and structured settlement transactions.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, as amended by the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA)
delays until 2015 the requirement that individuals maintain minimal essential health care coverage.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the tax credit for increasing research activities through 2010
modify the rate of such credit and make such modified credit permanent after 2010.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax exemption for structured settlement factoring transactions
to require that court orders approving such transfers include additional requirements.

topic.name: Environmental and Transportation Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating environmental impacts and transportation safety standards.

Document 1: ensures that DOT flight, duty, and rest requirements under that rule apply to all-cargo flights
applies to passenger flights
Document 2: amends the North American Wetlands Conservation Act
allow the non-federal share of the U.S. contribution to the costs of wetlands conservation projects
Document 3: prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency from requiring an NPDES permit
defines 'covered vessel' to mean a vessel that is less than 79 feet in length

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions on Security and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at enhancing security through counternarcotics efforts and promoting conservation in specific regions.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to include a report on interagency counternarcotics strategy for the region of Mexico, Central and South America, and the Caribbean
Requires federal assistance through current counternarcotics programs to be provided in the Region for operational, intelligence, and forensic support
Document 2: Amends the Highlands Conservation Act to extend funding for land conservation partnership projects
Programs to assist states, local government, and private landowners in the conservation of land and natural resources
Document 3: Amends the Horse Protection Act to replace the Designated Qualified Persons program responsible for inspecting horses for soring
The Department of Agriculture must establish a single Horse Industry Organization to ensure compliance with the Horse Protection Act

topic.name: Government Reform and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving the efficiency and effectiveness of government agencies and services.

Document 1: Chiropractic Care Available to All Veterans Act of 2009
requires a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care
Document 2: Government Reform Act of 2009
Establishes a Government Reform Commission to examine the current configuration of executive agencies
Document 3: Government Reform Act of 2011
Establishes a Government Reform Commission to review agency operational jurisdictions

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Biobased Products
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the development, deployment, and support of renewable energy technologies and biobased products.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to include facilities that convert renewable biomass into renewable chemicals
Makes renewable chemicals and biobased product manufacturing eligible for grant and loan guarantee assistance.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy to develop and publish near-, medium-, and long-term goals for the deployment of clean energy technologies
Requires annual reports to Congress on the technologies supported and on Corporation performance.

topic.name: Environmental and Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the preservation and management of natural resources and historical uses within specific areas.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to ensure that all pack and saddle stock uses within the Emigrant Wilderness are recognized as appropriate wilderness activities
Requires the USDA to continue to authorize commercial pack and saddle stock services and issue authorizations to provide commercial services for commercial stock operations.
Document 2: NOAA must establish five regions within the committee
develop grant proposals to fund projects that address the industry needs.

topic.name: Land Exchange and Trust Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts related to the exchange and conveyance of federal lands, particularly concerning tribal interests and conservation efforts.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Elko County, Nevada, all right, title, and interest of the United States in approximately 300 acres of land.
Holds in trust by the United States for the benefit and use of the Te-moak Tribe of Western Shoshone Indians of Nevada.
Document 2: Directs the Department of the Interior to quitclaim to the San Bernardino Valley Water Conservation District approximately 327 acres of identified federal land.
In exchange for such land accept from the Conservation District a conveyance of approximately 310 acres of its land.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey certain federal land in Pinal County, Arizona to Resolution Copper.
Requires preparation of an environmental review document to be used as the basis for all decisions under federal law related to the land exchange.
